success_factors: Workflow status is success with all expected nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy is appropriate for Pyomo and aligns with high import feasibility (0.9).
MCP plugin startup/health checks passed (service healthy, stdio transport).
Low intrusiveness risk and medium repository complexity made non-invasive wrapping feasible.
failure_reasons: Quality gap: original project tests were not executed successfully (passed=false with empty details), reducing validation confidence.
DeepWiki analysis failed, so architecture/context enrichment was incomplete.
Generated artifact metrics are inconsistent (0 LOC, 0 file size, 0 endpoints in generation summary vs populated endpoint list in service info), indicating reporting/instrumentation defects.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully; repository was imported via zip fallback (2352 files), which likely added overhead but improved robustness.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed with broad structural/dependency discovery; no entry points auto-detected despite known CLI modules, suggesting partial static-detection limits.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation completed and produced expected MCP files; however generation telemetry quality is low (zeroed LOC/size/tool count).
node_performance.test_time: MCP runtime health passed, but functional endpoint contract/smoke tests are missing; original project test result is effectively non-validated.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown due to missing metrics (reported as 0); cannot evaluate reliably.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown due to missing metrics (reported as 0); cannot evaluate reliably.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Likely moderate-to-high due to very large repository footprint; generated plugin footprint appears small but reported size telemetry is invalid.
